instName: ﻿Christian Brothers of the Society of Mary
instNote: ﻿This society, founded by Rev. Wm. Jos. Cheminade, honorary canon of the Metropolitan church of Bordeaux, and approved by his Holiness Gregory XVI, April 23d, 1839, has been established in Cincinnati, to be emplyed in the German schools in that city. Their Superior in this country is Rev. L. Meyer, who is assisted by Rev. E. X. Mauclere. The novitiate is at Nazareth, near Dayton, on a feritle and extensive farm.

instNote: ﻿Situated on a beautiful eminence, whithin a mile of the town, commands a picturesque view and is reputed a healthy place. Its extensive farm and woods afford most desirable advantages for healthful exercise for the boys. It combines the agremens of the country with the conveniences of the town. Terms—Board and Tuition quarterly...$20 00; Bed, if furnished by the establishment... 4 00; N. B. Only Catholic pupils are admitted.
